SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.name: Synthetic Habitat
SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.desc: A massive, self-contained artificial environment, often housing entire civilizations within its metallic shell.

SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.name: Shattered World
SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.desc: The fragmented remains of a planet destroyed by a cataclysmic event, its pieces now forming a hazardous but resource-rich asteroid field.

SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.name: Ringworld Fragment
SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.desc: A broken section of a colossal, ancient ring structure that once encircled a star, now tumbling through space.

SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.name: Ringworld
SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.desc: A complete, impossibly large artificial ring built around a star, with a habitable inner surface millions of times larger than a normal planet.

SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.name: Planetary Scaffold
SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.desc: A planet-wide construction grid, either in the process of building a world or left abandoned mid-construction.

SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.name: Dyson Sphere
SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.desc: A complete shell built around a star to capture its entire energy output. A sign of a truly advanced, likely transcendent, civilization.

SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.name: Dyson Swarm
SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.desc: A massive cloud of independent orbital habitats and solar collectors surrounding a star, representing an early stage of Dyson Sphere construction.

SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.name: Dyson Plate
SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.desc: A single, continent-sized solar collector in a tight orbit, a more modest and achievable form of stellar engineering.

SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.name: Stellar Engine
SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.desc: A megastructure designed to move an entire star system through the galaxy, often by directing stellar thrust.

SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.name: Protoplanetary Disk
SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.desc: A swirling disk of gas and dust around a young star, where new planets are in the process of forming.

SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.name: Asteroid Field
SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.desc: A dense collection of asteroids, often the remnants of a failed planet or a primordial feature of the system.

SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.name: Debris Field
SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.desc: The wreckage of ancient battles or industrial accidents, a dangerous but potentially salvageable graveyard of ships and stations.

SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.name: Dense Dust Cloud
SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.desc: An interstellar cloud of gas and dust so thick it obscures vision and interferes with sensors, often a stellar nursery.

SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.name: World-Engine Core
SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.desc: The exposed, still-humming core of a planet-sized terraforming machine from a bygone era, radiating immense energy.

SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.name: Silent Oracle
SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.desc: A colossal, inert computational device of unknown purpose, possibly a repository of lost knowledge or a dormant super-intelligence.

SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.name: Gravitic Waveguide Nexus
SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.desc: A central hub of the ancient FTL network of The First, now dangerously unstable and leaking spatial distortions.

SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.name: Extinct Biosphere Archive
SPECIAL_PLANET_TYPES.desc: A preserved, dead ecosystem encased in a planetary-scale containment field, like a silent, ghostly museum.
